metadata.description: Merge multiple video clip files into a single output using FFmpeg. Supports simple concatenation and crossfade transitions. Validates clip compatibility before merging.

input.name: clip_list
input.description: Ordered list of clip file paths to merge.

input.name: merge_strategy
input.description: Merge strategy. One of: - "ffmpeg-concat": Simple concatenation using FFmpeg concat demuxer with -c copy (no re-encoding). Fastest. Requires identical codecs/resolution/framerate. - "crossfade": Concatenation with crossfade transitions using xfade filter. Requires re-encoding.

input.name: crossfade_duration
input.description: Duration of crossfade in seconds. Only used with "crossfade" strategy.

input.name: output_path
input.description: Output file path for the merged video.

output.description: Merged video file containing all input clips in order.

step.name: Validate Clip Existence
step.description: Verify all input clip files exist and are readable.

actions.action: For each clip in clip_list: - Verify file exists at the specified path - Verify file is readable and non-zero size - Verify file has a valid video extension (.mp4, .mov, .webm) If any clip is missing: HALT with list of missing files. If fewer than 2 clips: HALT "At least 2 clips required for merging."

step.name: Probe Clip Metadata
step.description: Extract technical metadata from each clip using ffprobe.

actions.action:
For each clip, run: ffprobe -v quiet -print_format json -show_streams -show_format "{clip_path}"
Extract: - resolution: width x height - frame_rate: r_frame_rate (e.g., "24/1") - codec: codec_name (e.g., "h264", "vp9") - pixel_format: pix_fmt (e.g., "yuv420p") - duration: in seconds - audio_codec: if audio stream present - audio_sample_rate: if audio stream present

step.name: Verify Compatibility
step.description: Ensure all clips have matching resolution, frame rate, and codec.

actions.action:
Use the first clip as reference. Compare all subsequent clips:
CHECK: Resolution matches (width x height) - If mismatch with ffmpeg-concat strategy: HALT "Resolution mismatch. Use crossfade strategy or re-render." - If mismatch with crossfade strategy: WARN, use first clip's resolution as target.
CHECK: Frame rate matches - If mismatch: HALT "Frame rate mismatch."
CHECK: Codec matches - If mismatch with ffmpeg-concat: HALT "Codec mismatch. Use crossfade strategy for re-encoding." - If mismatch with crossfade: WARN, will re-encode to common codec.

step.name: FFmpeg Concat
step.description: Generate concat demuxer file and execute simple concatenation.

actions.action: Create temporary concat list file with content: file '{absolute_path_to_clip_1}' file '{absolute_path_to_clip_2}' ...

Execute FFmpeg: ffmpeg -y -f concat -safe 0 -i "{concat_list_path}" -c copy "{output_path}"
This performs stream copy (no re-encoding) -- fastest possible merge.

step.name: Crossfade Merge
step.description: Execute merge with crossfade transitions using xfade filter.

actions.action: Build FFmpeg filter_complex chain: - For N clips, create N-1 xfade filter stages - Calculate offset for each transition: offset_i = sum(durations[0..i]) - (crossfade_duration * i) - Apply same logic for audio using acrossfade filter

Execute FFmpeg (example for 3 clips): ffmpeg -y \ -i "{clip1}" -i "{clip2}" -i "{clip3}" \ -filter_complex " [0:v][1:v]xfade=transition=fade:duration={xf_dur}:offset={offset1}[v01]; [v01][2:v]xfade=transition=fade:duration={xf_dur}:offset={offset2}[vout]; [0:a][1:a]acrossfade=d={xf_dur}[a01]; [a01][2:a]acrossfade=d={xf_dur}[aout] " \ -map "[vout]" -map "[aout]" \ -c:v libx264 -preset medium -crf 18 \ -c:a aac -b:a 192k \ "{output_path}"
Output duration = sum of inputs - (crossfade_duration * (clip_count - 1))

step.name: Verify Output
step.description: Verify the merged output file is valid and duration is correct.

actions.action: Check output file exists and has non-zero size. Run ffprobe on output to verify: - Valid video container - Resolution matches input clips - Video and audio streams present

Calculate expected duration: - ffmpeg-concat: sum of all clip durations - crossfade: sum of durations - ((num_clips - 1) * crossfade_duration) Verify actual duration is within 1 second of expected. If mismatch > 1s: WARN

step.name: Cleanup and Report
step.description: Remove temporary files and report the merge result.

actions.action: Remove temporary concat list file (if created)
Keep all source clips (never delete originals)
Report result: - output_path, file_size, duration, clip_count, strategy, resolution, codec

error.action: HALT: "FFmpeg is required but not found in PATH."
HALT: "Resolution mismatch. Clip '{clip}' is {res} but expected {expected}. Use crossfade strategy or re-render."
HALT: "Codec mismatch. Clip '{clip}' uses {codec} but expected {expected}. Use crossfade strategy."
HALT: "Frame rate mismatch. Clip '{clip}' is {fps}fps but expected {expected}fps."
HALT: "FFmpeg merge failed with exit code {code}. stderr: {output}"
HALT: "At least 2 clips required for merging. Received {count}."
